message:
feat(core,layout,wasm,svg): extend rendering pipeline and WASM API (+317 lines)

- fm-wasm/lib.rs: +73 lines expanding WASM rendering API
- fm-layout/lib.rs: additional layout computation refinements
- fm-render-svg/lib.rs: SVG rendering improvements
- fm-core/lib.rs: extend core types
- fm-cli/main.rs: CLI output adjustments
- Sync beads tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2nw.15","title":"Build automated feature parity verification against FrankenTUI","description":"# Automated Feature Parity Verification against FrankenTUI

Goal:
- Generate objective parity evidence that combines capability metadata, E2E telemetry, and proof artifacts into release consumable reports.

Scope:
- Build feature matrix generation from shared capability sources and runtime evidence.
- Compare reference and FM outputs semantically and perceptually with explicit confidence tags.
- Publish machine readable and human readable parity dashboards.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for matrix generation, scoring rules, and threshold evaluation.
- Integration tests for ingestion of reference capture plus CLI and WASM artifacts.
- E2E parity runs with deterministic replay and triage outputs.
- Structured logs must include feature id, support state, reference verdict, FM verdict, delta type, confidence, artifact links, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:26.913410616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.757022782Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.776755747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.120837335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.208363086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.866478867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.952552130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.036757681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.913294434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.550949443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.793558913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.674886281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Feature Parity Matrix
Automated verification against FrankenTUI:
1. All diagram types parsed
2. All node shapes rendered
3. All edge styles supported
4. All styling options work

### Automated Comparison
```bash
# scripts/parity-check.sh
for sample in corpus/*.mmd; do
  frankentui_output=$(frankentui render $sample)
  frankenmermaid_output=$(fm-cli render $sample)
  compare_outputs $frankentui_output $frankenmermaid_output
done
```

### Coverage Tracking
1. Feature checklist with pass/fail status
2. Percentage coverage metric
3. Regression detection on coverage drop

### Logging Requirements
- Detailed comparison trace
- Difference categorization (visual, structural, semantic)
- Prioritized gap list","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.2","title":"Extract minimap rendering from mermaid_minimap.rs into fm-render-term","description":"# Minimap Rendering Extraction

Extract the complete minimap functionality from FrankenTUI's mermaid_minimap.rs (1,474 lines) into frankenmermaid's fm-render-term crate.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_minimap.rs
- Lines: 1,474
- Purpose: Overview visualization of large diagrams with viewport indicator

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Minimap Computation
- Proportional scaling to fit overview area
- Node density-based detail level selection
- Viewport rectangle calculation
- Pan/zoom coordinate mapping

### 2. Rendering Modes
- Terminal minimap (block characters)
- Simple minimap (ASCII only)
- Colored minimap (highlight regions)

### 3. Viewport Interaction
- Viewport position calculation
- Click-to-navigate coordinate mapping
- Viewport size indicator

### 4. Performance Features
- Lazy rendering (only visible regions)
- Cached minimap with dirty tracking
- Progressive detail rendering

## Implementation Notes

The FrankenTUI minimap uses:
- Downsampling algorithms for node representation
- Edge simplification (straight lines only)
- Color quantization for terminal output

Key adaptations needed:
1. Remove ftui-style dependencies
2. Integrate with fm-layout positioned nodes
3. Support multiple output targets (term, SVG, canvas)
4. Add streaming/progressive rendering

## Acceptance Criteria

- All minimap modes from reference implemented
- Unit tests for scaling/viewport math
- Integration with fm-render-term
- Visual regression tests
- Performance: <50ms for 1000-node diagrams","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:27.959621218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:30.845005356Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extraction","minimap","render"],"comments":[{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Proportional scaling calculation correctness
2. Viewport rectangle computation for various zoom levels
3. Click coordinate mapping to diagram position
4. Detail level selection based on node density

### Integration Tests
1. Minimap renders correctly for diagrams of various sizes
2. Viewport indicator tracks main view accurately
3. Pan/zoom coordinate mapping is bijective

### Visual Regression Tests
Use golden output comparisons for:
- Small diagram minimap (< 10 nodes)
- Medium diagram (10-50 nodes)
- Large diagram (100+ nodes with clusters)

### Logging Requirements
- Structured trace of viewport calculations
- Render timing per detail level
- Memory usage for cached minimap","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.3","title":"Complete Sugiyama coordinate assignment with Brandes-Köpf algorithm","description":"# Sugiyama Coordinate Assignment Completion

Implement the complete Brandes-Köpf coordinate assignment algorithm from FrankenTUI's mermaid_layout.rs to achieve pixel-perfect layout parity.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs
- Lines: ~3,000 (coordinate assignment section)
- Algorithm: Brandes-Köpf (2001) with enhancements

## Current State

fm-layout has basic coordinate assignment but lacks:
- Proper block alignment
- Median positioning
- Compact horizontal assignment
- Type 1/Type 2 conflict resolution

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Vertical Alignment Phase
- Mark type-1 conflicts (where edges cross)
- Mark type-2 conflicts (long edges)
- Determine median alignment
- Handle multi-edge nodes

### 2. Horizontal Compaction Phase
- Block building (aligned node groups)
- Class separation computation
- Minimum width assignment
- Sink-based coordinate shifting

### 3. Four-Way Computation
- Upper-left alignment
- Upper-right alignment
- Lower-left alignment
- Lower-right alignment
- Final: median of four positions

### 4. Edge Straightening
- Long edge dummy alignment
- Port assignment
- Bend minimization

## Algorithm Properties (Alien-Artifact-Coding)

- Time complexity: O(n) where n = nodes + dummy nodes
- Space complexity: O(n)
- Determinism: Stable ordering guarantees identical output
- Proof: Each phase preserves layer assignment invariant

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI uses bidirectional sweep with:
1. LEFT_UP, RIGHT_UP, LEFT_DOWN, RIGHT_DOWN passes
2. Median of 4 positions for final coordinates
3. Compact representation via class graph

Critical: Preserve exact FrankenTUI behavior for determinism verification.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 4-way alignment implemented
- Visual parity with FrankenTUI reference
- Property tests for invariants
- Performance: <10ms for 100-node graphs
- Determinism proof (golden tests)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:44.540525239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.001816418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","layout","sugiyama"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.693988459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":109,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Algorithm Correctness Tests
1. Vertical alignment phase preserves layer assignment
2. Block building creates valid aligned groups
3. Class separation computation is correct
4. Horizontal compaction minimizes width

### Four-Way Computation Tests
1. Upper-left alignment produces valid coordinates
2. Upper-right alignment is mirror of upper-left
3. Lower-left alignment respects reversed layers
4. Lower-right alignment combines both mirrors
5. Final coordinate is median of four results

### Property Tests (proptest)
1. All node coordinates are finite
2. No horizontal overlaps within layers
3. Layer gaps are consistent
4. Total width is minimal for given constraints

### Determinism Tests
1. Same graph = same coordinates (100 runs)
2. Cross-platform determinism (Linux/macOS/WASM)
3. Floating-point consistency

### Golden Tests
Reference outputs for:
- Simple DAG (5-10 nodes)
- Complex DAG with long edges
- DAG with clusters
- Wide flat graph

### Logging Requirements
- Per-phase coordinate dump
- Block structure trace
- Class assignment decisions","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:53Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2nw.4","title":"Implement source span tracking through full pipeline","description":"# Source Span Tracking Pipeline

Implement comprehensive source span tracking from input text through IR to rendered output, enabling click-to-source and precise error highlighting.

## Overview

Every element in the pipeline should carry its originating source span:
- Parser: Token spans → AST spans → IR spans
- Layout: IR spans → LayoutNode spans
- Render: LayoutNode spans → Output element spans

## Key Features

### 1. Parser Span Tracking
- Character-level offset tracking
- Line/column computation
- Multi-span nodes (e.g., chained edges)
- Comment and whitespace preservation

### 2. IR Span Preservation
- IrNode.span_primary: Main declaration span
- IrNode.span_all: All mentions in source
- IrEdge.span: Edge declaration span
- IrLabel.span: Label text span

### 3. Layout Span Propagation
- LayoutNode inherits IR spans
- Dummy nodes carry long-edge span
- Cluster spans from subgraph blocks

### 4. Render Span Emission
- SVG: data-source-span attributes
- Terminal: span → position mapping
- WASM: span query API

### 5. Source Map Generation
- V3 Source Map format for web tooling
- Bidirectional lookup (source→render, render→source)
- Incremental source map updates

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI tracks spans via:
- Span { start: usize, end: usize, line: u32, col: u32 }
- Multi-span aggregation for complex nodes
- Lazy line/column computation

We need:
1. Consistent span representation across crates
2. Span merging utilities
3. Source map serialization
4. Query APIs for tooling

## Use Cases

1. Error highlighting: Show exact location of parse errors
2. Click-to-source: Click rendered node → jump to source
3. Hover info: Show source context on hover
4. Diff correlation: Map diff regions to source changes

## Acceptance Criteria

- Spans preserved through parse → layout → render
- Source map emission for SVG output
- WASM span query API
- <1% performance overhead
- Integration with fm-cli validate output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:03.139786220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:24.610016724Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","parser","source-map"],"comments":[{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":95,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Span Propagation Tests - verify span covers node/edge/subgraph declarations
2. Multi-span Tracking Tests - verify span_all collects all references
3. Layout Span Inheritance Tests - verify LayoutNode inherits IrNode spans

### Integration Tests
1. Full Pipeline Tests - Parse to Layout to Render with span assertions
2. Error Localization Tests - span points to exact error locations

### E2E Validation
1. Click-to-Source Tests - recover source span from rendered position
2. Unicode Tests - verify character-accurate boundaries

### Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON log entries with span fields
- Format: {stage, element_id, span: {start, end, line, col}}","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2nw.5","title":"Extract full terminal render capabilities from mermaid_render.rs","description":"# Terminal Render Full Extraction

Complete extraction of terminal rendering from FrankenTUI's mermaid_render.rs (9,352 lines) into fm-render-term.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs
- Lines: 9,352
- Purpose: Full-featured terminal diagram rendering

## Current State

fm-render-term has basic terminal rendering (~2,000 lines) but lacks:
- Full shape library (all 20+ node shapes)
- Advanced edge rendering (curves, routing)
- Style application (colors, bold, dim)
- Unicode box drawing optimization
- Cluster rendering with borders

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Shape Rendering (all shapes)
- Rectangle, rounded-rect, circle, ellipse
- Diamond, hexagon, parallelogram, trapezoid
- Stadium, subroutine, cylinder, database
- Double-circle, flag, note, document
- All asymmetric shapes

### 2. Edge Rendering
- Straight edges with arrow heads
- Orthogonal routed edges
- Curved edges (quadratic Bézier approximation)
- Edge labels (inline, above, below)
- Multi-segment long edges

### 3. Text Rendering
- Unicode-aware text wrapping
- Grapheme clustering for width calculation
- Truncation with ellipsis
- Vertical centering in shapes
- Label overflow handling

### 4. Style System
- ANSI color support (16, 256, true color)
- Bold, italic, underline, dim
- Background colors
- Style inheritance (class-based)
- Theme support

### 5. Cluster Rendering
- Cluster borders (box drawing)
- Cluster titles
- Nested cluster support
- Cluster padding and margins

### 6. Performance Features
- Dirty region tracking
- Incremental redraw
- Virtual scrolling for large diagrams

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI uses:
- ftui-render trait system for drawing
- Cell-based canvas with dirty tracking
- Pre-computed glyph widths

We need to:
1. Create standalone RenderSurface trait
2. Implement TerminalSurface with crossterm/termion
3. Add mock surface for testing
4. Preserve rendering fidelity exactly

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 20+ node shapes rendered correctly
- Edge routing matches reference
- Style application complete
- Visual regression tests
- Performance: 60fps for 500-node diagrams","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:21.366755973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:31:13.768759367Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extraction","render","terminal"],"comments":[{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (per shape)
Test each of 20+ node shapes:
- Rect, rounded-rect, circle, ellipse, diamond
- Hexagon, parallelogram, trapezoid, stadium
- Subroutine, cylinder, database, double-circle
- Flag, note, document, asymmetric shapes

### Edge Rendering Tests
1. Straight edge with all arrow types (-->, --o, --x, etc.)
2. Orthogonal routed edges with turns
3. Curved edges (Bezier approximation)
4. Edge labels (inline, above, below positions)
5. Multi-segment long edges spanning layers

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram rendering with mock terminal surface
2. Determinism: same input = same terminal output
3. Unicode box drawing correctness
4. Cluster border rendering with nested content

### Visual Regression
Golden output files for each diagram type + shape combination

### Logging Requirements
- Trace-level logs for shape drawing decisions
- Per-shape render timing
- Character cell usage statistics","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.6","title":"Implement adaptive quality degradation under compute pressure","description":"# Adaptive Quality Degradation under Compute Pressure (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Validate and calibrate degradation behavior against reference expectations while using shared bd-3uz degradation infrastructure.

Scope:
- Calibrate pressure tiers, operator ordering, and quality scoring for parity corpus scenarios.
- Verify user facing explainability and quality mode behavior under load.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for tier thresholds, operator scoring, and deterministic selection.
- Integration tests for parse, layout, render budget transitions.
- E2E stress scripts for CLI and WASM with repeat run determinism checks.
- Structured logs must include pressure signals, selected tier, operator chain, quality score, time saved, diagnostics, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:40.685241756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.324082246Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","performance","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.153473730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.491688165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.598196098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:50.820655394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:50.947665406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.056688220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":99,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Pressure signal detection on native (CPU, memory thresholds)
2. Pressure tier quantization (low/medium/high/critical)
3. Quality degradation operator correctness
4. Budget allocation across pipeline stages

### Integration Tests
1. Full pipeline under simulated high load
2. Graceful degradation sequence verification
3. Quality vs latency tradeoff validation
4. Recovery when pressure subsides

### E2E Stress Tests
```bash
# scripts/test-degradation.sh
# Generate increasing load until degradation triggers
for size in 100 500 1000 5000 10000; do
  fm-cli render --max-budget-ms=100 large_diagram_${size}.mmd
  # Assert output quality monotonically decreases, never crashes
done
```

### Logging Requirements
- Structured pressure readings at each checkpoint
- Degradation operator invocation trace
- Quality score at each stage
- Total budget consumed vs allocated","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.7","title":"Implement comprehensive determinism verification harness","description":"# Determinism Verification Harness (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Prove deterministic behavior against both internal invariants and reference parity expectations.

Scope:
- Build parity drift classification and tolerance policies on top of shared proof harnesses.
- Produce cross platform determinism evidence packages for CI and release gates.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for hash normalization and drift categorization logic.
- Integration tests for repeated parse, layout, render hash stability.
- E2E corpus replay across platforms with deterministic seeds.
- Structured logs must include run id, input hash, platform, stage hashes, drift class, tolerance rationale, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:57.189064124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.479806803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.353827912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.254202835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Property-Based Tests (proptest)
1. Parse-layout-render roundtrip produces identical output
2. Re-running layout with same IR = same coordinates
3. Rendering same layout = same pixel/character output
4. Hash of output is stable across runs

### Determinism Proof Infrastructure
1. Golden test corpus with checksums
2. CI job that runs 100x and asserts identical output
3. Cross-platform determinism (Linux/macOS/WASM)

### Invariant Tests
1. Layout coordinates are always finite and bounded
2. No overlapping node bounding boxes (unless intentional)
3. Edge routes don't cross node interiors
4. Cluster boundaries contain all member nodes

### Replay Tests
1. Capture full trace of layout computation
2. Replay from trace produces identical result
3. Binary diffable trace format for debugging

### Logging Requirements
- JSON trace of all layout decisions
- Hash checkpoint at each pipeline stage
- Structured diff when non-determinism detected","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:51Z"}]}

+fn count_known_node_spans(layout: &fm_layout::DiagramLayout) -> usize {
+    layout
+        .nodes
+        .iter()
+        .filter(|node| !node.span.is_unknown())
+        .count()
+}
+
+fn count_known_edge_spans(layout: &fm_layout::DiagramLayout) -> usize {
+    layout
+        .edges
+        .iter()
+        .filter(|edge| !edge.span.is_unknown())
+        .count()
+}
+
+fn count_known_cluster_spans(layout: &fm_layout::DiagramLayout) -> usize {
+    layout
+        .clusters
+        .iter()
+        .filter(|cluster| !cluster.span.is_unknown())
+        .count()
+}
+
 fn collect_structural_diagnostics(parsed: &fm_parser::ParseResult) -> Vec<ValidationDiagnostic> {
     let mut diagnostics = Vec::new();
